sanskrit: ज्ञेयं यत्तत्प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वाऽमृतमश्नुते । अनादिमत्परं ब्रह्म न सत्तन्नासदुच्यते ॥
hindiTranslation: जो ज्ञेय है, उसपरमात्मतत्त्व को मैं अच्छी तरहसे कहूँगा, जिसको जानकर मनुष्य अमरताका अनुभव कर लेता है ।  वह ज्ञेयतत्त्व अनादि और परम ब्रह्म है ।  उसको न सत् कहा जा सकता है और न असत् ही कहा जा सकता है ॥
englishTranslation: I will now tell you what is to be known. Knowing it, one attains freedom. It is beginningless supreme reality, beyond existence and nonexistence.
essence: The highest reality frees you precisely because it outgrows every label.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna says he will explain the highest reality that can be known. Knowing it brings freedom. It is beginningless, supreme, and beyond ordinary ideas of real and unreal.
simpleInsight: There is relief in knowing the deepest truth cannot be boxed into simple labels.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is frozen and Krishna begins the teaching on field and knower. After describing the qualities that prepare a seeker, Krishna now points to the highest object of knowledge itself: the beginningless supreme reality that ends confusion.
modernRelevance: You are trying to name something that changes your whole life — but every label feels too small. Some truths are not objects you can pin down; they must be known directly.
translationLiteral: ज्ञेयम् (what is to be known) / यत् (which) / तत् (that) / प्रवक्ष्यामि (I will clearly tell) / यत् (which) / ज्ञात्वा (having known) / अमृतम् (freedom / immortality) / अश्नुते (attains) / अनादिमत् (beginningless) / परम् (supreme) / ब्रह्म (supreme reality) / न (not) / सत् (existent) / तत् (that) / न (not) / असत् (nonexistent) / उच्यते (is called)
insight: It shifts the mind from arguing about appearances to seeking direct understanding of what underlies them.

quoteVariants.text: Know the supreme reality. It has no beginning, and it escapes all labels.
Know the supreme reality. Beyond being, beyond nonbeing.
What you seek cannot be named as real or unreal.
